<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
I've reviewed the codebase and am ready to summarize where polling is used.

Real API polling occurs in three places:

- Order placement: polls `getOrderViewData` every 3 seconds until the order is accepted, missed, or cancelled
- Subscription offer purchase: same 3-second polling pattern
- Map view: polls driver location every 5 seconds

UI-only timers exist in the home menu carousel, subscription offers page, and animation test, but these do not fetch data.</details></think>

_**Agent (default)**_

Yes — there is real API polling in a few places, plus some UI-only timers.

**API / data polling**

1. **Order status after checkout** (`api_controller.dart`) — every **3s** calls `getOrderViewData` until the order is `accept` / `delivered` / `missed` / `cancelled`, then cancels the timer.
2. **Subscription offer purchase** (`api_controller.dart`) — same **3s** order-status poll after buying from a subscription offer.
3. **Driver location on the map** (`osm_map.dart`) — every **5s** calls `getDriverLocationAndUpdateMap` while the map is open.

**Not data polling (UI timers)**

- `home_menu_page.dart` — auto-scrolls the category list
- `subscription_offers_page.dart` — carousel timer is **commented out**
- `animation_test.dart` — test scroll timer

So the live polling paths are order-status waiting and driver location tracking.

---

_**User**_

for the first 2, we can do what we did in sanmiwago_kiosk: C:\GitHub-Repos\Winnie\setup-intent-kiosk-pos\sanmiwago_kiosk

We did that for subscriptions orders so no. 2 must be covered directly and for no. 1 we will use similar approach. let's first of all plan that and then we will move on to the implementation and then to the polling of driver location thingy.

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
I have enough context to draft a solid plan.

The plan will cover both placeOrder and subscription flows together. PusherService will be ported from the kiosk but limited to order-update events, skipping the kiosk channel. Waiting logic will move fromApiController timers into WaitingPage as a StatefulWidget, mirroring the kiosk pattern. Timeout will default to four minutes plus a one-minute buffer, with optional idle-time fetching if the endpoint exists.

A `missedOrderTimeLimit` constant will default to four minutes. The checkout flow will trigger PusherService connection before posting the order, then navigate to WaitingPage with the order ID and flow type. WaitingPage will listen to the status stream and fall back to a direct data fetch on timeout.

WaitingPage will receive orderId and flow metadata as arguments. It will call OrderWaitHelper.navigateForStatus and disconnect Pusher on exit. The Timer.periodic calls in both methods of api_controller will be removed. Credentials will be prefetched on the splash screen after controllers initialize. Fallback credentials will be mapped to the user app's EnvType.</details></think>

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
I have everything needed. I am creating the plan now.

The plan covers both placeOrder and subscription flows. Driver location polling is deferred.

Architectural choices include porting a simplified PusherService for order updates only. WaitingPage will become a StatefulWidget that owns its listener and timeout. A shared OrderWaitHelper will manage navigation across two flows. Timer.periodic will be removed from the API controller. Prefetching will happen on the splash screen. ensureConnected will run before checkout and subscription redemption.

WaitingPage will receive orderId, flow type, and subscription offer details via constructor arguments. The kiosk timeout UI will be a full-screen message.

The user app currently polls indefinitely. The plan will add a timeout plus a one-shot order-view, matching the kiosk pattern.</details></think>

_**Agent (default)**_

<tool-use data-tool-type="unknown" data-tool-name="create_plan">
<details>
<summary>Tool use: **create_plan**</summary>


Parameters:

```json
{
  "name": "Pusher order waiting",
  "overview": "Replace the 3-second order-status polling in &#96;placeOrder&#96; and &#96;buyItemsFromSubscriptionOffer&#96; with the same lazy Pusher &#96;order-update&#96; approach used in sanmiwago_kiosk. Driver-location polling stays out of scope.",
  "plan": "# Pusher order-status waiting (user app)\n\nMirror [sanmiwago_kiosk](C:/GitHub-Repos/Winnie/setup-intent-kiosk-pos/sanmiwago_kiosk)’s subscription waiting design for **both** regular checkout and subscription redeem. Same backend channel/events; no FCM changes; driver map polling deferred.\n\n## Current vs target\n\n| Today | Target |\n|-------|--------|\n| &#96;Timer.periodic(3s)&#96; → &#96;getOrderViewData&#96; in [&#96;api_controller.dart&#96;](lib/controllers/api_controller.dart) (~1010 and ~3254) | Pusher event on &#96;order-update&#96; (event name = status) |\n| Infinite poll | Timeout = &#96;missedOrderTimeLimit&#96; (default **4 min**) + **1 min** buffer, then **one** &#96;getOrderViewData&#96; |\n| [&#96;WaitingPage&#96;](lib/views/pages/order_placed_result/waiting_page.dart) is dumb UI | Page owns listen + timeout + navigate + disconnect |\n| No Pusher in user app | Port slimmed kiosk &#96;PusherService&#96; (order-update only) |\n\n## Architecture\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;mermaid\nsequenceDiagram\n    participant Splash\n    participant CheckoutOrRedeem\n    participant Api as ApiController\n    participant Pusher as PusherService\n    participant Wait as WaitingPage\n    participant Backend\n\n    Splash-\u003e\u003ePusher: prefetchCredentials()\n    CheckoutOrRedeem-\u003e\u003ePusher: ensureConnected() before POST\n    CheckoutOrRedeem-\u003e\u003eApi: placeOrder / buyItemsFromSubscriptionOffer\n    Api-\u003e\u003eBackend: POST place-order / create-offer-subscription-order\n    Api-\u003e\u003ePusher: setWaitingOrderId(orderId)\n    Api-\u003e\u003eWait: navigate with orderId + flow args\n    Backend--\u003e\u003ePusher: order-update event accept/missed/cancelled/delivered\n    Pusher--\u003e\u003eWait: statusStream\n    Wait-\u003e\u003eWait: navigate outcome + disconnect\n    Note over Wait: On timeout: disconnect, one GET order-view, then navigate or timeout UI\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n**Connect vs filter (same as kiosk):** subscribe to shared &#96;order-update&#96; without &#96;order_id&#96;; after placement, filter by &#96;order_data.order_id&#96;. Buffer terminal status if the event arrives before the waiting page mounts.\n\n## What to port / add\n\n**Dependency:** &#96;pusher_channels_flutter: ^2.5.0&#96; in [&#96;pubspec.yaml&#96;](pubspec.yaml).\n\n**New files** (adapted from kiosk; drop kiosk-channel / header-image code):\n\n- [&#96;lib/constants/pusher_constants.dart&#96;](lib/constants/pusher_constants.dart) — cluster &#96;ap2&#96;, channel &#96;order-update&#96;, terminal events &#96;{accept, delivered, missed, cancelled}&#96;\n- [&#96;lib/services/pusher/pusher_service.dart&#96;](lib/services/pusher/pusher_service.dart) — singleton: &#96;prefetchCredentials&#96;, &#96;ensureConnected&#96;, &#96;setWaitingOrderId&#96;, &#96;statusStream&#96;, &#96;terminalStatusFor&#96;, &#96;disconnect&#96;\n- [&#96;lib/services/pusher/pusher_fallback_creds.dart&#96;](lib/services/pusher/pusher_fallback_creds.dart) — env-scoped keys when &#96;fetch-pusher-info&#96; fails (map user-app [&#96;EnvType&#96;](lib/utils/enums.dart) similarly to kiosk)\n- [&#96;lib/services/pusher/order_wait_helper.dart&#96;](lib/services/pusher/order_wait_helper.dart) — shared terminal check + navigation for **two flows**:\n  - **cart:** &#96;OrderPlacedPage&#96; / &#96;OrderRejectedMissedPage&#96; (+ existing accept side effects: &#96;getUserPoints&#96;, &#96;clearOrderData&#96;)\n  - **subscription:** &#96;SubscriptionOrderPlacedPage&#96; / &#96;SubscriptionOrderRejectedMissedPage&#96; (+ mark offer &#96;isSubscribed&#96;)\n- Small config holder (e.g. on existing constants or a tiny &#96;AppConfig&#96;) for &#96;missedOrderTimeLimit&#96; default &#96;Duration(minutes: 4)&#96;. Optional later: wire &#96;/idle-time&#96; like kiosk; not required for v1.\n\n**API constant:** &#96;fetchPusherInfoEndpoint = \"/fetch-pusher-info\"&#96; in [&#96;api_constants.dart&#96;](lib/constants/api_constants.dart).\n\n## Flow changes\n\n### Prefetch\nIn [&#96;splash_screen.dart&#96;](lib/views/pages/launch/splash_screen.dart) after controllers/base URL are ready: &#96;unawaited(PusherService.instance.prefetchCredentials())&#96; — in-memory only, no socket yet.\n\n### Before POST\n- Checkout place tap ([&#96;checkout_page.dart&#96;](lib/views/pages/checkout/checkout_page.dart) / wherever &#96;placeOrder&#96; is triggered): &#96;unawaited(PusherService.instance.ensureConnected())&#96; then place.\n- Subscription redeem (path into &#96;buyItemsFromSubscriptionOffer&#96;): same &#96;ensureConnected()&#96; before POST.\n- On placement **failure**: &#96;disconnect()&#96; so the socket does not linger.\n\n### After successful POST ([&#96;api_controller.dart&#96;](lib/controllers/api_controller.dart))\n1. Remove both &#96;Timer.periodic&#96; blocks.\n2. &#96;PusherService.instance.setWaitingOrderId(orderId)&#96;.\n3. Navigate to &#96;WaitingPage&#96; with args: &#96;orderId&#96;, flow (&#96;cart&#96; | &#96;subscription&#96;), and subscription-only fields (&#96;offerId&#96;, &#96;offerRestaurantId&#96;, invoice if available).\n4. Keep existing cart &#96;LocalDatabase.updateCartOrderId&#96; for FCM cart-clear (unchanged).\n\n### WaitingPage → StatefulWidget\nListen / timeout / exit logic lives here (kiosk pattern), not in &#96;ApiController&#96;:\n\n1. Check &#96;terminalStatusFor(orderId)&#96; (race buffer), else listen to &#96;statusStream&#96;.\n2. Start one-shot timeout timer (&#96;missedOrderTimeLimit + 1 min&#96;).\n3. On terminal status → cancel timers/sub → &#96;disconnect&#96; → &#96;OrderWaitHelper.navigateForStatus(...)&#96; (only if still on waiting route).\n4. On timeout → disconnect → single &#96;getOrderViewData&#96; → if terminal, navigate; else show simple timeout UI + leave (same idea as kiosk’s timeout screen).\n5. &#96;dispose&#96; always cancels subscription/timer and disconnects if still waiting.\n\n## Explicitly out of scope\n\n- Driver location polling in [&#96;osm_map.dart&#96;](lib/views/pages/location/osm_map.dart)\n- FCM / [&#96;notification_service.dart&#96;](lib/services/notification_service.dart) changes (accept notification → Orders tab stays as-is)\n- Backend changes (existing &#96;order-update&#96; contract)\n- Kiosk &#96;kiosk&#96; channel / header image\n\n## Verification\n\n- Place cart order → WaitingPage → accept/miss/cancel navigates correctly without 3s polls in network log\n- Redeem subscription offer → same\n- Kill restaurant / no event until timeout → one &#96;order-view&#96; then correct page or timeout UI\n- Leave/fail placement → no lingering Pusher connection\n- Confirm &#96;fetch-pusher-info&#96; works on the env under test; fallback key used if API fails\n",
  "todos": [
```

_**Agent (default)**_

Agreed — and the plan already matched that; I just made it explicit.

**Startup:** only `prefetchCredentials()` (HTTP + in-memory key). No socket.

**Connect:** only on place/redeem tap via `ensureConnected()`, right before the POST — same lazy timing as kiosk’s Redeem path for order waiting.

**Disconnect:** when waiting ends, times out, or placement fails. No always-on connection.

Kiosk’s early/ongoing connect was for the header-image channel. We don’t have that here, so we won’t open Pusher at app start.

---

_**User**_

Pusher order-status waiting (user app)

Implement the plan as specified, it is attached for your reference. Do NOT edit the plan file itself.

To-do's from the plan have already been created. Do not create them again. Mark them as in_progress as you work, starting with the first one. Don't stop until you have completed all the to-dos.

---

_**Agent (default)**_

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
Locating the navigate helper and checking placeOrder to determine where to insert ensureConnected.

Placing ensureConnected at the start of placeOrder and buyItemsFromSubscriptionOffer is more reliable than tapping the button directly. This covers all call sites including retries from the rejected page. Disconnection will happen on failure paths.

Checking how to retrieve the invoice number for subscription orders. The invoice ID likely comes from getOrderViewData, but Pusher events only provide status updates. WaitingPage may need to fetch the invoice from orderController.selectedOrderData or call getOrderViewData once.

The kiosk passes invoiceId directly from the placement response. Cart OrderPlacedPage reads from orderController.selectedOrderData, so the invoice must be fetched before navigating there. Since Pusher only delivers status updates, OrderWaitHelper should call getOrderViewData once before navigating to OrderPlacedPage. WaitingPage could also fetch it before navigation.

OrderPlacedPage consumes selectedOrderData, so cart acceptance requires getOrderViewData before navigation. Subscription orders pass orderId and invoiceNumber as constructor arguments from polled values. The best approach is to hydrate data inside OrderWaitHelper.navigateForStatus before routing by status. Alternatively, WaitingPage could fetch the view data first. Missed and cancelled subscription pages also need orderId and invoiceNumber.

WaitingPage will call getOrderViewData once before navigating on every terminal status. OrderWaitHelper will then route using the freshly hydrated selectedOrderData. This ensures cart orders receive their invoice and subscription orders receive their invoice number without extra logic.

The timeout path already fetches the order view data. When Pusher reports an accepted status, the flow disconnects, fetches the data, and then navigates. A buffering race is handled by WaitingPage checking terminalStatusFor against the buffered event. I am also verifying whether placeOrder contains early returns before the POST call that would prevent ensureConnected from firing.

ensureConnected should run immediately before the POST call in placeOrder, after validation but before the request. Calling it at the method start is also acceptable since the connection should be ready by the time the POST returns. I am also checking whether the subscription placement response includes an invoice_number field.</details></think>

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
Beginning implementation now. Creating all required files and wiring them together.

Deciding where to place the `OrderWaitFlow` enum. `ensureConnected` will run right before the actual POST call. Disconnection logic is being wired into both `placeOrder` and `buyItemsFromSubscriptionOffer` failure paths. `WaitingPage` will accept a `cart` or `subscription` flow argument.

Defining `WaitingPage` fields: `orderId`, `flow`, optional `offerId`, `offerRestaurantId`, and `invoiceNumber`. For subscription acceptance, the invoice will come from `getOrderViewData` or the widget parameter. Timeout leaves will route cart flows to `HomeMenuPage` or `BottomNavBarPage`, and subscription flows to `Subscriptions` or the bottom navigation bar.

Both cart and subscription timeouts will route to BottomNavBarPage using offAll. A MyButton widget is needed for the timeout UI.

Adding `missedOrderTimeLimit` to `AppConstants`. Mapping fallback credentials by environment type: merge maps to one key, production variants map to another, and dev environments fall back to the merge key. `PusherService` will omit the kiosk channel for a simpler disconnect. `getOrderViewData` comments will be updated. After a successful `placeOrder`, the order ID will be extracted from the response and saved to `LocalDatabase`.

Cart orders will pass the order ID directly to `WaitingPage` with `OrderWaitFlow.cart`. Subscription orders will extract `order_id` and `invoice_id` from the API response, falling back to `invoice_number` if needed, then pass those values along with the offer details. <summary>Tool use: **edit_file_v2**</summary>

</details></tool-use>

_**Agent (default)**_

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
A bug was found in `_handleTerminalStatus`. When called from a timeout with `disconnectFirst: false`, `_isHandlingExit` is already true.

The early return only triggers when `disconnectFirst` is true. The timeout path therefore reaches `_handleTerminalStatus` with `_isHandlingExit` already set. Re-entering that flag is redundant because the timeout already fetched the order view.

Next steps include wiring the API controller by removing timers, adding `ensureConnected`, `setWaitingOrderId`, navigation with arguments, and disconnecting on failure. Splash prefetch and a `MyButton` constructor parameter fix are also planned.</details></think>

_**Agent (default)**_

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
Only informational messages appeared. Now verifying how BaseClient.get handles failed response processing.

The kiosk ignores events arriving before `setWaitingOrderId` because `_waitingForOrderId` is null. This creates a race window where early events are missed.

The buffered terminal status only re-emits when `waitingForOrderId` matches. This suggests the buffer exists for a specific edge case, though its exact purpose is unclear.

The buffered status cannot be set without a matching order ID. This suggests the re-emit in `setWaitingOrderId` may be redundant or indicate a kiosk bug. The buffer appears designed for a specific event flow.

The buffer ensures the waiting page receives the terminal status once it mounts. Events arriving before `setWaitingOrderId` are lost, matching kiosk behavior. The timeout fallback covers missed events.

Buffering events by order ID before `waitingForOrderId` is set is out of scope; kiosk behavior will be matched instead.

Remaining `Timer.periodic` polling for orders must be removed. Wire-flows will be marked complete and verified.

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
A subtle bug exists in `_handleTerminalStatus` when called from a timeout with a terminal status found.

The timeout path sets `_isHandlingExit = true` before calling `_handleTerminalStatus`, but a concurrent timeout that hasn't found a terminal yet may still see that flag as true and skip handling.

The timeout path is safe. Re-fetching order data is harmless since the timeout already holds it. Double-navigation is prevented by the exit flag. Buffered post-frame calls are also handled correctly.

The timeout-to-terminal path is confirmed safe. If the terminal status is missing, the exit flag remains set and the timeout UI appears after Pusher disconnects. The unused `flow` parameter warning in `navigateAfterTimeout` will be fixed. All to-do items are now marked complete.

Confirmed the WaitingPage route name matches its class name. Verified that clearing order data before navigating to OrderPlacedPage is safe, since that page reads selectedOrderData for invoice display.</details></think>

_**Agent (default)**_

Order-status polling is replaced with lazy Pusher waiting for both cart and subscription flows.

**What changed**
- Added `pusher_channels_flutter` plus slim `PusherService` (order-update only), fallback creds, and `OrderWaitHelper`
- Splash: `prefetchCredentials()` only (no socket)
- `placeOrder` / `buyItemsFromSubscriptionOffer`: `ensureConnected()` before POST; `setWaitingOrderId` + navigate on success; `disconnect()` on failure
- `WaitingPage` is now stateful: listens to Pusher, 4min+1min timeout, one-shot `order-view` fallback, timeout UI
- Removed both `Timer.periodic` order-status loops

**Still polling (out of scope)**
- Driver location on `osm_map.dart` (next when you’re ready)

Worth a quick device check: place a cart order and redeem a subscription item, confirm accept/miss/cancel navigate without 3s `order-view` spam, and that a long wait hits the timeout path.

_**Agent (default)**_

Driver location polling uses:

**`POST /driver-live-tracking-fetch`**

Defined as `ApiConstants.driverLocationEndpoint` in [`api_constants.dart`](lib/constants/api_constants.dart).

Called from [`location_controller.dart`](lib/controllers/location_controller.dart) with body:

```dart
{"driver_id": driverId, "order_id": orderId}
```

[`osm_map.dart`](lib/views/pages/location/osm_map.dart) hits that every **5 seconds** via `getDriverLocationAndUpdateMap()`.

---

_**User**_

Event : 'driver-location-update'
Channle : 'driver-live-tracking-' . $order_id

$notfn_data['order_id']   = $order_id;
$notfn_data['driver_id']  = $driver_id;
$notfn_data['lat']        = $lat;
$notfn_data['lon']        = $lon;
$pusher->trigger(array('driver-live-tracking-' . $order_id), 'driver-location-update', $notfn_data);

This is what the backend dev sent. so I believe the event is going to be for each order specifically so we can start listening when we go to that page, and stop listening when we levae the page?

---

_**Agent (default)**_

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
The user wants to confirm whether the Pusher channel should be scoped per order and bound to page lifecycle events.

I'll confirm the per-order channel and event name against the backend contract. I'll also check how this fits with the existing OSM map polling setup. No implementation yet unless requested.</details></think>

_**Agent (default)**_

Yes — that’s the right reading of the backend contract.

| Piece | Value |
|--------|--------|
| **Channel** | `driver-live-tracking-{order_id}` (one channel per order) |
| **Event** | `driver-location-update` |
| **Payload** | `order_id`, `driver_id`, `lat`, `lon` |

So the lifecycle can be page-scoped:

1. **Enter map page** → ensure Pusher connected → subscribe to `driver-live-tracking-{orderId}` → on `driver-location-update`, update the marker (same as today’s poll handler, without the HTTP loop).
2. **Leave / dispose** → unsubscribe that channel (and disconnect if nothing else needs Pusher — e.g. you’re not mid order-wait).

That’s cleaner than the shared `order-update` wait channel: you don’t need to filter by `order_id` on a big bus; the channel itself scopes the stream.

One nuance vs checkout waiting: order-wait uses a **shared** channel + local filter; driver tracking uses a **per-order** channel. Same `PusherService` / creds, different subscribe target and lifetime.

Also keep a **one-shot** (or first) `GET/POST /driver-live-tracking-fetch` for the initial pin before the first Pusher event, then rely on events after that — same pattern as “connect then wait for updates.”

---

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
Extending PusherService.

Adding a driver location channel subscription. Events will stream live location updates. Subscription will attach on the map page and detach on exit. Disconnection logic must preserve order-wait channels. The service will be refactored to support multiple channels like the kiosk pattern.

A lightweight model will carry driver location data. Pusher constants will gain a driver channel prefix and event name. PusherService will track driver subscription state and provide methods to subscribe and stop tracking. The disconnect method will preserve driver tracking when order-wait channels remain active.

The map widget will stream driver locations via Timer.periodic. Connection setup will include the driver tracking channel. Disconnect logic will be split into separate methods: one for order-wait channels and one for driver tracking.

Internal socket disconnection will only occur when no channels are active. Credential errors will reset driver subscription flags. Connection state changes and successful subscriptions will resubscribe the driver channel. The channel name will be `driver-live-tracking-$order_id`. The update payload will contain order_id, driver_id, latitude, and longitude.

The DriverLocationUpdate model will be placed in pusher_service.dart or a dedicated model file. The osm_map widget will drop its polling timer. It will fetch the initial driver location once via API when the map is ready, then switch to Pusher streaming. Map updates will reuse the same logic as the poll path. On dispose, the widget will stop driver tracking and cancel the subscription. The driver tracking subscription will be triggered upon entering the map page or when the map becomes ready.

A shared `_applyDriverLocation` helper will handle both the initial API fetch and the Pusher stream updates.</details></think>

_**Agent (default)**_

Extending `PusherService` for the per-order driver channel, then wiring the map page to subscribe on enter and unsubscribe on leave.

_**Agent (default)**_

Driver location polling is replaced with page-scoped Pusher updates.

**Backend contract**
- Channel: `driver-live-tracking-{order_id}`
- Event: `driver-location-update`
- Payload: `order_id`, `driver_id`, `lat`, `lon`

**Behavior**
1. Map ready → one-shot `POST /driver-live-tracking-fetch` for the initial pin  
2. Then subscribe to `driver-live-tracking-{orderId}` and update the marker/road on each event  
3. Leave/dispose → unsubscribe + drop the socket if order-wait isn’t using it  

**Files**
- [`pusher_constants.dart`](lib/constants/pusher_constants.dart) — channel prefix + event name  
- [`pusher_service.dart`](lib/services/pusher/pusher_service.dart) — `ensureDriverTrackingSubscribed` / `stopDriverTracking` / `driverLocationStream`  
- [`osm_map.dart`](lib/views/pages/location/osm_map.dart) — removed the 5s `Timer.periodic`  

Order-wait on `order-update` and driver tracking can coexist; leaving the map won’t tear down an active wait socket, and finishing a wait won’t tear down an open map subscription.

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
Yes. Those four terminal statuses exit the waiting page for both cart and subscription flows.</details></think>

_**Agent (default)**_

Yes. For **both** cart and subscription waiting, we only act on those four terminal statuses:

| Status | Cart | Subscription |
|--------|------|--------------|
| `accept` / `delivered` | `OrderPlacedPage` (+ points, clear cart) | `SubscriptionOrderPlacedPage` (+ mark subscribed) |
| `missed` | `OrderRejectedMissedPage(isRejected: false)` | `SubscriptionOrderRejectedMissedPage(...)` |
| `cancelled` | `OrderRejectedMissedPage(isRejected: true)` | `SubscriptionOrderRejectedMissedPage(...)` |

Anything else (`new`, etc.) is ignored on Pusher and only resolved via the timeout → one-shot `order-view` fallback (or the timeout UI if still non-terminal). Same set as the old polling branches and the kiosk.
